message:
fix(tests): resolve 3 Coverage Suite failures from test-split cascade (#9319)

PR #9302 split useVersionCheck.test.tsx and useCachedData.test.ts into
focused modules but left a stale helper reference and two brittle
`vi.doMock` patterns that race with `vi.resetModules()` on the first
test of a file in CI.

Three failures from Coverage Suite run #1164:

1. useVersionCheck-fetch-update.test.tsx:700 — `ReferenceError:
   isReleasesApiCall is not defined`. The helper moved to the
   parse-provider split; inline a 3-line copy in the fetch-update split
   so the suite is self-contained.

2. useCachedData.security-gitops.test.ts — `useCachedSecurityIssues
   fetcher: agent kubectl finds privileged containers` was failing with
   `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'ok')` because the
   first-test-of-the-file `vi.doMock('../mcp/shared', ...)` didn't
   overwrite the hoisted `vi.mock` factory before the lazy re-import
   pulled `clusterCacheRef.clusters = []`, sending the fetcher down the
   REST fallback path.

3. useCachedData.progressive-gpu.test.ts — `fetchClusters prefers local
   agent clusters over backend` failed the same way (`No clusters
   available`).

Fix for 2 & 3: expose a mutable `mockClusterCacheRef` via
`vi.hoisted()`, point the hoisted `vi.mock('../mcp/shared', ...)`
factory at it, and have the two failing tests mutate
`.clusters` directly instead of re-mocking. `beforeEach` resets the
array so tests stay independent. Leaves every other `vi.doMock` call
in these files untouched — those run after the registry has warmed up
and work correctly.

Also repair the unrelated `ReferenceError: done is not defined` in
useAIPredictions.test.ts:488 (`const _done = false` declared a
never-assigned binding; rename to `let done` and reference it to
silence the unused-var lint).

Fixes #9305

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
